title: Best Costume Design: Ranking Every Nominee of the 98th Academy Awards
text: A comprehensive ranking of all 2026 Oscar costume design nominees. Avatar: Fire and Ash by Deborah L. Scott ranks fifth, with the reviewer acknowledging the interesting garments worn by Na'vi species but noting that most outfits are computer-generated, making it difficult to rank above films with physically made costumes. Marty Supreme by Miyako Bellizzi ranks fourth, with the fashion effectively conveying each character's personality and background. The costumes appear worn before and lived in, showing Marty's varying success levels through wardrobe choices. Sinners by Ruth E. Carter ranks third, with Carter's work featuring believable, lived-in outfits set in a historical period. Despite characters having different circumstances, they all look sharp in designs that feel authentically chosen rather than like stock film uniforms. Hamnet by Malgosia Turzanska ranks second, with the costumes feeling anthropologically specific and creating a sense of a time capsule as opposed to a romanticized depiction. They're designed to make characters resemble historical figures brought to life. Frankenstein by Kate Hawley ranks first, combining lived-in authenticity with lavish moments, featuring costumes that feel like they have been worn for years. The reviewer predicts Frankenstein will win this category.
